image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: From grocery truck to fighter jet: Inside Ali Khamenei's final journey
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists create smartwatch-like plant wearables that let farmers monitor crops in real time, detecting drought, disease and nutrient problems days before plants show visible signs of stress
item.description: British farmers are on the verge of integrating innovative plant wearables to enhance crop monitoring. These tiny sensors affixed to leaves and stems can detect distress signals in plants days before visible symptoms appear. Powered by moisture evaporation, the devices continuously operate, distinguishing between water and salinity stress while tracking growth alterations. A network of these wearables could yield unparalleled insight into field conditions from a plant's perspective.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: 'Spain was very generous today': Trump softens rhetoric after Madrid highlights Nato spending
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 4 US cops helped India's Chandrakant Patel in a decade-long U visa fraud in Louisiana: Fake robbery, fake police report, real visa
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: One Walmart heiress owns a $300 million superyacht, while her billionaire sister owns a Texas ranch 13 times the size of Washington DC, where helicopters herd cattle
item.description: Walmart heiresses Ann Walton Kroenke and Nancy Walton Laurie are icons of immense wealth, each showcasing their lifestyle choices through extravagant assets. While Ann is the proud owner of the sprawling Waggoner Ranch, one of the largest in the U.S., Nancy indulges in her luxurious $300 million superyacht, Kaos, which has drawn the ire of environmental activists.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Iran's funeral politics: Khamenei's burial becomes message to region and rivals
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Target vs target: What US hit in Iran, and what Tehran struck in response
item.description: The fragile ceasefire between the United States and Iran has collapsed after fresh military strikes. Both nations launched attacks, expanding the conflict across the Gulf region. Iran targeted US air defence systems and facilities in several Middle Eastern countries. The United States struck approximately ninety military sites across Iran. These exchanges marked the biggest breach of the truce since April.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: He manages a $1.4 trillion empire and wears a $140 Swiss watch. Now Stephen Schwarzman has a $400 million superyacht with a glass-bottom pool, helipad and fold-down balconies
item.description: Stephen Schwarzman, the chairman of Blackstone, has made headlines by purchasing a lavish superyacht named Destiny. This stunning 101.2-meter craft, which set him back around $400 million, comes from the renowned builder Feadship. Among its luxurious features are a glass-bottom pool and a helipad. Notably, Schwarzman started his remarkable career with a modest $400,000 investment, and he is often seen sporting affordable Swatch watches.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Indonesia's new Scorpène submarine can stay at sea for 80 days: Here's what makes it special
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Rishabh Agarwal opens up about rejecting $1 million proposal from Meta: The offer was higher....
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Egypt is building an entire desert city from scratch, crowned by a 1,263 foot skyscraper
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Indonesia is building a brand-new capital in the Borneo jungle to replace sinking Jakarta
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'This is awful': MP Rupert Lowe criticises 'changing demographics' after Muhammad tops UK baby names for third consecutive year
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Sheriff's locked drawer reveals evidence linked to missing drug-seizure cash
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists find nanoplastics in Antarctic soil for the first time, including tyre-wear particles, raising concerns that tiny plastic pollution can travel through the atmosphere to Earth's most remote places
item.description: In a groundbreaking study, scientists have identified nanoplastics in the pristine soils of Antarctica for the very first time. These microscopic plastic particles journey through the atmosphere, reaching even the most isolated regions. Analysis of soil samples revealed contamination from tyre wear and everyday plastic products. It suggests that both local human activities and far-reaching atmospheric movements contribute to this alarming trend, underscoring the widespread impact of plastic pollution worldwide.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Researchers discover cave with rare cave pearls in Vietnam's Phong Nha-Ke Bang National Park
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Mosque effigy atop Northern Ireland bonfire sparks outrage; man arrested after anti-Muslim display
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists have filmed a rare 125-million-year-old goblin shark alive in its natural deep-sea habitat for the first time, revealing a new depth record and a wider Pacific range
item.description: For the very first time, scientists have captured live footage of goblin sharks in their deep-sea environment, revealing new insights into the species. This remarkable finding extends their known depth by almost 700 meters. Observations from two separate instances identified these elusive sharks in previously unknown Pacific Ocean habitats, complemented by archived footage from 2019 that documented an unrecognized encounter. This groundbreaking research broadens the goblin shark's recognized range significantly.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: ‎Meet Bakari, the 21-year-old gorilla chosen to lead Disney's famous troop after the death of its longtime silverback
item.description: Bakari, a 21-year-old male gorilla, has joined Animal Kingdom as the new troop leader. He arrived from the Saint Louis Zoo as part of a conservation program. Bakari takes over leadership after the passing of Gino, the former troop leader. Gino had led the gorilla troop for nearly three decades at the park. This transfer marks a new chapter for the park's western lowland gorillas.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Lake Powell and Lake Mead are both running dangerously low, and scientists now say one radical idea could help: drain most of Lake Powell and store the water in Lake Mead instead
item.description: A Colorado River expert suggests moving water from Lake Powell to Lake Mead. This proposal addresses ongoing drought conditions impacting western United States water supplies. Lake Powell's dam infrastructure limits usable water storage, while Lake Mead offers greater capacity. The plan involves gradual water transfer and potential exposure of Glen Canyon. This strategy aims for long-term water management solutions for millions.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk
